parsed_review.summary: CP00-644 is a descriptor-only Risk C closeout pack (122 units, RP20.P09.M04.S13-RP20.P09.M10.S10) for the RP20 Data Room/VDR P09 review closeout tail. Read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack is internally consistent and its (pending) production_ready posture is supported by implementation, tests, and local/contract validation. Verified: (1) The CP644 pack binding in packages/data-room/src/registry.js matches the baseline, the contract projection in contracts/data-room-vdr-core-contract.json (cp644_pack_binding/cp644_requirements/cp644_no_write_attestation/cp644_p09_review_closeout_tail_descriptor), the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, and next-pack-queue.json (live_latest CP00-644, next CP00-645). (2) Deliverable distribution computes deterministically to claude_review:18, implementation:78, security_audit:12, test:6, ui:6, hermes_evidence:2 = 122, from the P09_REVIEW row definitions (QUESTION=10, EXTENDED=20, FULL=22, SECONDARY_TAIL=EXTENDED.slice(12)=8) and the title->type classifier in service.js; this matches registry expected_deliverable_counts and the manifest (doubled 36/156 due to two included_units arrays). The 7 micro-phase sections (M04-M10) and row counts/keys are asserted by validateDataRoomVdrCoreCp644Coverage. (3) freezeCp644Result sets all 7 executes_p09_review_*_runtime flags plus the no-write attestation (no product-state writes, no audit/permission writes, no object storage, no Hermes runtime receipt, synthetic_only/no_real_data) to false; validators and model tests assert each boundary and contract-projection deep-equality. (4) command-evidence.json records 19 commands all exiting 0 (node --check, rp20 validator, node --test, npm test/validate/build, git diff --check); readiness-report auth preflight passed with valid JSON (no auth failure, no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call output). (5) Claude is kept read-only and non-approving (claude_is_final_approval:false, allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob, forbidden_review_evidence includes claude_final_approval_claim/enterprise_trust_claim/source_mutation_claim), and Hermes patterns are descriptor/receipt-only (hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). The chain CP00-643 -> CP00-644 -> CP00-645 (next_subphase RP21.P00.M00.S01) is consistent. The pack's evidence artifacts (command-evidence/construction-inspection/claude-review-result/adjudication) correctly show in_progress/pending pre-closeout state awaiting this review; no premature production_ready or enterprise-trust claim is asserted. Human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust/runtime-opening claim; this review is advisory read-only evidence, not final approval. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
